mainmatter.p: The Wednesday Seminars are the Department’s main seminar series.

The talks are given by distinguished external speakers as well as members of the department. They are targeted at anyone with a background in computing, but students in particular. Topics span the full range of computer science and beyond.

frontmatter.title: An invitation to univalent foundations of mathematics

mainmatter.p: Voevodsky’s univalent foundations and homotopy type theory comprise a new and radical approach to the foundations of mathematics in which the structural properties of equality are extended to every possible form of equivalence and symmetry, leading to an expanded universe of discourse in which ordinary sets and algebraic structures exist harmoniously alongside infinite-dimensional spaces. In this talk, I will expose the basic grammar and vocabulary of the new univalent foundations — and explain how they shed light on basic problems in theoretical computer science and the specification of computer programs.
